channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Reform UK suspends campaign manager over racist and antisemitic social media posts
item.description: Reform UK has suspended Adam Mitula, the interim campaign manager for the Gorton and Denton by-election, after offensive comments he made on social media were revealed. The remarks, which reportedly i...

item.title: Tyra Banks Faces Backlash Following Netflix Documentary, But Does She Care?
item.description: Tyra Banks, the famous supermodel and host of *America’s Next Top Model*, has come under intense scrutiny following the release of Netflix’s *Reality Check: Inside America’s Next Top Model*. The docum...

item.title: U.S. Suspends Visa Processing for Citizens of 75 Countries Amid Stricter Screening
item.description: In a significant move, the U.S. State Department has suspended both immigrant and non-immigrant visa processing for citizens of 75 countries, effective January 21, 2026. The indefinite suspension appl...

item.title: Snoop Dogg Performs Private Set at Swansea Business Ahead of First Visit as Swansea City Co-Owner
item.description: American rapper Snoop Dogg surprised staff at the Au Vodka headquarters in Swansea by performing a private, invitation-only concert in the company’s canteen. The event took place ahead of his first of...

item.title: Hong Kong Protests Panama’s Takeover of Canal Ports Amid Legal and Geopolitical Dispute
item.description: Hong Kong has formally lodged a strong protest against Panama after the Panamanian government took control of two key container ports located along the Panama Canal. The ports had been operated for mo...

item.title: Highlights from the BRIT Awards 2026: Dua Lipa and Harry Styles lead stunning performances
item.description: The BRIT Awards 2026, held at Manchester's Co-op Live Arena, featured explosive performances from major stars, including Dua Lipa and Harry Styles. Dua Lipa surprised the audience with a dramatic perf...

item.title: Court hears allegations of prolonged domestic abuse preceding wife's suicide
item.description: Christopher Trybus, from Swindon, Wiltshire, is on trial at Winchester Crown Court accused of the manslaughter of his wife, Tarryn Baird, who died at the age of 34 in November 2017. The prosecution al...

item.title: 23 Prisoners Escape in Puerto Vallarta Amid Violence Following Death of Cartel Leader El Mencho
item.description: Mexican authorities are searching for 23 inmates who escaped from a prison in Puerto Vallarta during a surge of violence triggered by the death of Nemesio Oseguera Cervantes, known as 'El Mencho,' lea...

item.title: Scientists Develop Method to Convert Methane into Valuable Medicines and Chemicals
item.description: A team of scientists has achieved a significant breakthrough by finding a way to transform methane, a key component of natural gas, into valuable chemical building blocks for medicines and other produ...

item.title: Farage Claims Foreign-Born Voters Influenced Manchester By-Election Result Amid Allegations of Fraud
item.description: Nigel Farage has alleged that Reform UK lost the Gorton and Denton by-election due to foreign-born voters influencing the result. The former Ukip leader contended that his party was victorious among B...

item.title: Full List of Winners at the 2026 Brit Awards as Olivia Dean, Sam Fender, and Geese Take Top Prizes
item.description: The 2026 Brit Awards are being held at the Co-Op Live arena in Manchester on 28 February. For the first time in the award show’s history, it takes place outside London. Hosted by comedian Jack Whiteha...

item.title: Lorry Driver Pleads Guilty After Mother Dies From Falling Crane Equipment
item.description: A 71-year-old lorry driver, Kevin Miller from King's Lynn, Norfolk, has admitted causing the death of 30-year-old Rebecca Ableman, who was fatally injured by loose crane equipment while pushing her tw...

item.title: Figure Skating at the 2026 Winter Olympics: Key Moments and Future Impact
item.description: The 2026 Winter Olympics in Milano Cortina was filled with drama, surprises, and a new level of pressure in figure skating. With high stakes in every discipline, the event showed a clear shift in how ...

item.title: Two British Tourists Charged After Valentine's Day Stabbing in Venice
item.description: Two British cousins, aged 19 and 21, have been arrested and charged with attempted murder following a stabbing incident in Venice on Valentine’s Day. According to Italian authorities, the attack took ...

item.title: Man, 49, dies in tragic skydive incident at Devon airfield
item.description: A 49-year-old man has tragically died during a skydive at Dunkeswell Aerodrome, near Honiton, Devon. Police were called to the scene at around 13:00 GMT on Saturday, where emergency services confirmed...

item.title: Tottenham Face Relegation Battle Amid Poor Form, Injuries and Financial Concerns
item.description: Tottenham Hotspur are facing a serious relegation battle after a heavy 4-1 defeat to Arsenal in the north London derby left them just four points above the Premier League relegation zone. Under interi...

item.title: Christian Horner Denies Verstappen Family Role in His Red Bull Departure
item.description: Christian Horner has publicly addressed the circumstances surrounding his departure from Red Bull in 2025, firmly denying that Max Verstappen or his family played any role in his exit. Speaking to Net...

item.title: Man in court for defacing Winston Churchill statue in London
item.description: A man has been charged with criminal damage after graffiti was sprayed on the statue of Sir Winston Churchill in Parliament Square, central London. The graffiti, which included messages such as 'Never...

item.title: European Leaders Mark Fourth Anniversary of Ukraine War as Prospects for Peace Remain Distant
item.description: On the fourth anniversary of Russia’s invasion of Ukraine, European leaders gathered in Kyiv in a show of solidarity with President Volodymyr Zelenskyy and the Ukrainian people. The ceremony in Maidan...

item.title: David Lammy Admits Court Backlog May Persist Despite Planned Justice Reforms
item.description: In this episode of Newscast, Adam Fleming and Chris Mason interview Deputy Prime Minister David Lammy about the government’s proposed justice reforms and the ongoing challenges facing the UK court sys...
